settings.title: Our Spring Single-Origin Has Landed
settings.excerpt: A bright, citrus-forward Ethiopian lot is now pouring on bar through the end of June.
settings.body:
We taste a lot of coffee to choose just one. This spring it was an easy call: a washed Yirgacheffe with notes of bergamot, white peach, and a clean honeyed finish.

It is on as a pour-over and as a single-origin espresso, so you can taste how the roast shifts across brew methods. Our baristas are happy to walk you through both.

Where this coffee comes from

Yirgacheffe sits in the highlands of southern Ethiopia, one of the places where coffee first grew wild. This lot was washed, which means the fruit was stripped from the beans before drying, and that process is what gives the cup its clean, tea-like clarity. The bergamot note people keep mentioning is typical of the region, not something we added or imagined.

Brewed as a pour-over, it leans floral and delicate, with the peach showing up as it cools. Pulled as espresso, the same beans turn denser and the honeyed finish comes forward. Tasting them back to back is the easiest way to understand how much brew method changes a coffee.

How we would order it

  • Start with the pour-over if you want the brightest, most aromatic version.
  • Try the espresso if you prefer a sweeter, more concentrated cup.
  • Skip the milk this time, so nothing covers the citrus and stone fruit.

Stop by before the end of June while the lot lasts. Once these bags are gone we move on to the next harvest, so this exact cup will not be back.
